title: Fix webpack runtime error — incomplete node_modules stub in worktree
root_cause: The worktree at /worktrees/191/ has a fake/stub node_modules containing only stripped versions of 'next' (missing dist/shared/, dist/lib/, dist/client/, etc.) and 'styled-jsx'. These stubs shadow the full Next.js 15.1.0 installation in the parent workspace's node_modules, causing webpack to fail with 'Module not found: Can't resolve ../shared/lib/utils' and related errors.
infrastructure_changes: Remove stub node_modules directory from worktree
Run npm install to create a complete node_modules installation

steps.detail: Verify that node_modules/next/dist/shared/ is missing from worktree, and that parent workspace has the full installation at /Users/kevinliu/.appfactory/workspaces/kids-education-app-v4/node_modules/next

Delete /worktrees/191/node_modules entirely so Node.js module resolution falls back to the parent workspace's full installation. The parent has 546 packages at 516MB, matching the same package.json (same git repo).
Run npm run build. If it fails because webpack cannot find parent node_modules (e.g. Next.js resolves modules only relative to the project root), fall back to running npm install instead.

steps.action: Start dev server and verify pages
steps.detail: Start dev server on port 3042. Check HTTP response codes for /, /activities/explore-cards, /activities/listen-find, /parent. All must return 200, not 500.

Open a fresh browser (empty IndexedDB). Navigate to /. Should redirect to /onboarding. Enter child name → select age group → enter 4-digit PIN → confirm PIN → reach home screen showing child's name and activity buttons.
verification_points: Activity buttons (Explore Cards, Listen & Find, Matching Pairs, Song Time) are visible

steps.detail: Navigate to each activity page and confirm no 500 error. /activities/explore-cards shows vocabulary cards. /activities/listen-find shows game UI. /parent shows PIN gate.
Run npm test to verify all 207 unit tests still pass after the fix.
Commit with descriptive message explaining WHY (corrupted node_modules stub shadowing full installation). Write .af/tasks/191/pr-body.md.

expected_tests: All 207 existing unit tests must pass
npm run build exits with code 0

risk_areas.risk: Next.js webpack might not resolve modules from parent directory if it uses absolute paths for resolve.modules
risk_areas.mitigation: If build still fails after removing stub, run npm install to create full local installation

risk_areas.risk: Parent node_modules might have cached/stale packages that differ from worktree package-lock.json
risk_areas.mitigation: If npm test shows version-related failures, run npm install in worktree
risk_areas.probability: Low — same git repo, same package.json

risk_areas.risk: Other worktrees might also be affected by the same stub node_modules issue
risk_areas.mitigation: Out of scope — only fix worktree 191 as per task

persistence_strategy: N/A — this task fixes infrastructure, not data persistence. App uses IndexedDB (browser-local) for all data, which survives page refresh.
error_handling_approach: N/A — this task fixes infrastructure. Existing components already have try/catch with user-facing error messages (verified in code review).
rationale: The root cause is entirely in the node_modules directory (untracked, in .gitignore). No source files need to be changed. The fix is purely infrastructure: remove the incomplete stub and restore proper module resolution.

errors: Module not found: Can't resolve '../shared/lib/utils' in node_modules/next/dist/pages/_app.js
Module not found: Can't resolve '../shared/lib/constants' in node_modules/next/dist/pages/_document.js
Module not found: Can't resolve '../server/get-page-files' in node_modules/next/dist/pages/_document.js
Module not found: Can't resolve '../server/htmlescape' in node_modules/next/dist/pages/_document.js
Module not found: Can't resolve '../lib/is-error' in node_modules/next/dist/pages/_document.js
build_failure_details.cause: node_modules/next/dist/ only has compiled/, pages/, server/ — missing shared/, lib/, client/, build/, esm/, etc.
